limitations: v0.1 supports adjacent same-team action pairs only.
Does not infer tactical intent, planned combination, or causation.
runtime_contract.name: action_chain

parameters.description: Maximum elapsed provider event time between adjacent actions.
parameters.name: maximum_action_gap_seconds

parameters.description: Number of ordered actions in the chain. v0.1 supports adjacent pairs.
parameters.name: chain_length

runtime_contract.purpose: Join ordered same-team action anchors into a bounded event-time action chain.

limitations: Uses provider events for action identity and nearest tracking frame for alignment.
Does not prove controlled reception, intent, optimality, or tactical role.
runtime_contract.name: action_event_anchor

parameters.description: Provider event action type to expose as anchors.
parameters.name: action_type

runtime_contract.purpose: Expose provider event rows as typed action anchors with frame alignment.

limitations: Depends on canonical pitch coordinates and known pitch width.
runtime_contract.name: ball_lateral_fraction
runtime_contract.purpose: Normalize ball distance from pitch centerline toward either touchline.

limitations: Compares declared numeric evidence values only.
Does not infer why the change happened or whether it was tactically caused by the anchor event.

runtime_contract.purpose: Compare before and after numeric evidence values for the same anchor under declared change thresholds.

Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, causation, or defensive intent.
runtime_contract.name: controlled_line_break_episode

parameters.description: Distance from the supplied line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
parameters.name: line_buffer_m

runtime_contract.purpose: Evaluate whether a controlled pass carried a receiver from not-yet-beyond a supplied observed line at release to beyond that same line at controlled reception.

limitations: Uses event data for candidate passes and tracking data for release/reception proof.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, or decision quality.

parameters.description: Minimum receiver-control dwell for controlled reception.
parameters.name: minimum_receiver_dwell_seconds

parameters.description: Optional provider event-type filter for controlled-pass anchors.
parameters.name: event_type_filter

runtime_contract.purpose: Identify completed pass actions where event data supplies the candidate passer/receiver and tracking proves physical release plus controlled reception.

limitations: Models only an observed geometric defensive line under declared thresholds.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.

limitations: Goalkeepers are excluded from the outfield centroid.

limitations: No pass probability.

parameters.description: Retain corridors by relation destination side relative to the ball side.

limitations: Classifies observed outfield players only; it does not prove complete active-player lane coverage.

limitations: Observed geometric local count only; does not infer pressure or tactical role.

limitations: Requires provider event linkage for v0.1; tracking-only redirection candidates are not modeled.

Does not infer tactical intention, third-man combination, pass optimality, or decision quality.

Does not prove the pass alone caused every opponent to be bypassed.

limitations: Does not infer intent, optimality, or missed opportunity.
